payload.title: Standard Hospital Birth Registration (Within 21 Days)
payload.description: For births in registered hospitals within BBMP limits. Hospital submits digital registration via eJanMa portal. Parents receive SMS confirmation. Certificate can be downloaded online or collected from BBMP zonal office.
payload.applicable_to: Hospital births within 21 days of birth date

payload.title: Home Birth Registration (Within 21 Days)
payload.description: Parents must visit BBMP zonal office with parents' Aadhaar cards, address proof from birth time period, and two witness declarations from neighbors or relatives who knew about the birth. Medical attendant certificate if available.

payload.title: Delayed Hospital Birth Registration (21 to 30 Days)
payload.description: Hospital can still submit digitally but late fee of Rs 2 applies. Parents should follow up with hospital administration to ensure submission.
payload.applicable_to: Hospital births between 21 and 30 days after birth

payload.title: Late Birth Registration (30 Days to 1 Year)
payload.description: Requires late registration affidavit, witness documentation, and payment of Rs 5 penalty. Application submitted at BBMP zonal office with supporting evidence.
payload.timeline_days: 21 days under SAKALA Act for delayed cases
payload.applicable_to: Births registered between 30 days and 1 year after birth
payload.penalties: Additional affidavit and witness documents required

payload.title: Very Late Birth Registration (Beyond 1 Year)
payload.description: Requires magistrate approval, additional affidavits, witness documentation, and potentially judicial orders. Timeline extends to 6 to 12 months. Fee is Rs 10 plus court-related costs.
payload.applicable_to: Births registered more than 1 year after birth date

payload.title: Minor Birth Certificate Correction
payload.description: Spelling variations, missing initials, or typographical errors can be corrected through notarized affidavit and supporting ID submitted directly to BBMP office. Examples: Aanand to Anand, missing middle initial.
payload.penalties: Only ONE correction allowed per certificate

payload.title: Major Birth Certificate Correction
payload.description: Complete name alterations, surname additions, or fundamental identity changes require Gazette notification, newspaper publications in both Kannada and English, and potentially court-level approval for individuals over 15 years.
payload.applicable_to: Complete name changes, surname additions, identity modifications
payload.fees: Rs 3000 to 8000 including Gazette and newspaper costs
payload.penalties: Only ONE correction allowed; irreversible if misclassified

payload.title: Name Inclusion on Initially Blank Birth Certificate
payload.description: Birth certificates can be registered initially as Male Child or Female Child without a name. Parents have one year to add the name without additional burden. After one year, formal correction process with Gazette notification is required.
payload.timeline_days: Same as registration if within 1 year
payload.fees: No fee if within 1 year; Gazette costs if beyond 1 year
payload.penalties: After 1 year, escalates to major correction procedure

payload.title: Duplicate Birth Certificate Request
payload.description: If original certificate is lost or damaged, apply for duplicate through eJanMa portal online verification or visit BBMP zonal office with ID proof and application letter.
